compiled_state.title: As a developer, gate-2d and gate-2b read commands.format_check and skip gracefully when absent
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Modify gate-2d-dependency-check.sh and gate-2b-blast-radius.sh to read the commands.format_check config key. When present, invoke it; when absent, emit [DSO WARN] and exit 0.

**Why**: Gate scripts currently invoke format checking unconditionally, causing failures or Python-specific output for non-Python projects.

**Scope**:
- IN: gate-2d-dependency-check.sh and gate-2b-blast-radius.sh changes; [DSO WARN]+skip guard
- OUT: onboarding writes, validate.sh, auto-format.sh, other scripts beyond these two gates

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, both gate-2d-dependency-check.sh and gate-2b-blast-radius.sh with commands.format_check=prettier --check invoke prettier --check
  ← Satisfies: "gate-2d and gate-2b read commands.format_check; emit [DSO WARN] and skip when absent"
- When this story is complete, both gate scripts with no commands.format_check key emit [DSO WARN] commands.format_check not configured and exit 0
  ← Satisfies: "gate-2d and gate-2b emit [DSO WARN] and skip when absent"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing covering: key present, key absent (WARN+skip)

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Both scripts share the same WARN+skip pattern — implement consistently (inline, no premature helper abstraction)
- [Residual References] Before implementation, grep for all scripts/hooks invoking format checking (ruff check, prettier --check, etc.) beyond gate-2b and gate-2d. Known candidates: validate-phase.sh, merge-to-main.sh, smoke-test-portable.sh. If others are found, expand scope or note them for story S6 (residual callers audit)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
